seoTitle: Change Permissions From Edit to View in Microsoft Loop
metaDescription: Lock a shared Microsoft Loop page to read-only by changing its link permissions from Can Edit to Can View, protecting it from accidental changes.
intro: If you need to share a finished page or template without risking accidental edits, Microsoft Loop lets you restrict a shared link to view-only access. This guide walks through generating a link, adjusting its permission settings, and confirming that viewers can no longer modify the page.

steps.heading: Open the page and access sharing
steps.body: Open your target Loop page canvas, then click the primary Share button in the top right corner.

steps.heading: Open link settings
steps.body: Select the Page Link option from the dropdown, then click the settings gear icon to access more link options.

steps.heading: Change the permission level
steps.body: Open the Edit dropdown under More Settings and change the selection from 'Can edit' to 'Can view.'

steps.heading: Apply and copy the new link
steps.body: Click Apply to lock in the change, then copy the fresh view-only link to share with your intended audience.

steps.heading: Verify the restriction worked
steps.body: Check the live page as a restricted viewer to confirm they can't modify text blocks or tables.

faq.question: Will changing link permissions affect people who already have edit access?
faq.answer: Link-level permission changes generally apply to that specific shared link, so existing collaborators added directly to the workspace may retain their separate access level.

faq.question: Can I switch a view-only link back to editable later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reopen the same link settings and change the permission back to 'Can edit' at any time.

faq.question: Does restricting a link affect the whole workspace or just that page?
faq.answer: This method restricts the specific page link, not the entire workspace, so other pages retain their own separate permission settings.

faq.question: Why do some guests still seem to have edit access after I changed the link?
faq.answer: This can happen if there's a workspace-wide permission conflict overriding the page-level setting, so it's worth checking broader sharing settings if the restriction isn't sticking.
